nodes.name: 美洲
nodes.summary: 美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联9种鸟类，包括疣鼻棲鴨、Pied-billed grebe、Pale-vented pigeon等9种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联1443种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等1443种鸟类。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 自然系统第十版
nodes.summary: 自然系统第十版是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联25种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、黑嘴樹鴨、加拿大雁等25种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 林鹳
nodes.summary: 林鹳（Wood stork，学名 Mycteria americana），分类上属于鹳形目，并归入鹳科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联5个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括湿地、海岸湿地、河口等4项，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、猎捕压力。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 河口
nodes.summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

nodes.name: 海岸湿地
nodes.summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

nodes.name: 卡尔·林奈
nodes.summary: 卡尔·林奈是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联38种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、角叫鴨、白臉樹鴨等38种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 鹳形目
nodes.summary: 鹳形目（Ciconiiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.name: 北美洲
nodes.summary: 北美洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联35种鸟类，包括紅嘴樹鴨、黑嘴天鹅、加拿大雁等35种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鹳科
nodes.summary: 鹳科（Ciconiidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.name: 佛罗里达州
nodes.summary: 佛罗里达州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联12种鸟类，包括Black francolin、赤颈鸊鷉、Dark-billed cuckoo等12种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The wood stork (Mycteria americana) is a large wading bird in the family Ciconiidae (storks)

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The wood stork (Mycteria americana) is a large wading bird in the family Ciconiidae (storks)

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The wood stork (Mycteria americana) is a large wading bird in the family Ciconiidae (storks)

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Originally described in 1758 by Carl Linnaeus, this stork is native to the subtropics and tropics of the Americas where it persists in habitats with fluctuating water levels

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is the only stork species that breeds in North America

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: |year=2017 |title=Genetic differentiation and historical demography of wood stork populations in Brazilian wetlands: implications for the conservation of the species and associated ecosystems |url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/aqc.2791 |journal=Aquatic Conservation: Marine and Freshwater Ecosystems |language=en |volume=27 |issue=6 |pages=1313–1324 |doi=10.1002/aqc.2791 |bibcode=2017ACMFE..27.1313M |issn=1052-7613|hdl=11336/42115 |hdl-access=free }} Birds that breed in west-central Brazil often disperse to southern Brazil and northern Argentina after breeding.{{Cite journal |last1=Del Lama |first1=S

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: In freshwater habitats, it primarily nests in forests dominated by trees of the genus Taxodium (in the US), while in estuaries, it generally nests on trees in the mangrove forests.{{cite journal |last1=Rodgers |first1=J

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: In freshwater habitats, it primarily nests in forests dominated by trees of the genus Taxodium (in the US), while in estuaries, it generally nests on trees in the mangrove forests.{{cite journal |last1=Rodgers |first1=J

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: |year=1996 |title=Nesting habitat of wood storks in north and central Florida, USA |journal=Colonial Waterbirds |volume=19 |issue=1 |page=1 |doi=10.2307/1521802 |jstor=1521802}} To feed, the wood stork uses freshwater marshes in habitats with an abundance of Taxodium trees, while in areas with mangrove forests, it uses brackish water

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: This is a recovery from its former status as endangered, which it held from 1984 to 2014 because of a decline in its population caused by habitat loss and drought. Similarly, in the state of Santa Catarina, Brazil, its decline seems to have been reversed: after an absence between the late 1960s and the mid-1990s, the species is now again regularly encountered there, in particular in the Tubarão River region. It is likely that the Paraná River region's wetlands served as a stronghold of the species, from where it is now re-colonizing some of its former haunts

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Hunting and egg-collecting by humans has been implicated as a factor in the decline of South American wood storks
